text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Margaret Chen
MRN: [REDACTED]
DOB: [REDACTED]  AGE: 62  SEX: Female
ATTENDING PHYSICIAN: [Thoracic Oncology / Interventional Radiology]
ACCESSION NUMBER: SP-[REDACTED]
SPECIMEN RECEIVED: [DATE]
REPORT DATE: [DATE]
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN SUBMITTED:
  A. CT-guided core needle biopsy, left upper lobe lung mass

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CLINICAL INFORMATION:
History of a 62-year-old female, former smoker (25 pack-year history), presenting with an incidentally identified 4.2 cm spiculated left upper lobe lung mass on staging CT, associated with mediastinal and left supraclavicular lymphadenopathy. PET-CT demonstrating FDG-avid primary mass (SUVmax 12.4) and nodal disease. Procedure performed under CT fluoroscopic guidance for tissue diagnosis. Rule out malignancy.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PROCEDURE:
CT-guided percutaneous core needle biopsy, left upper lobe mass
Sampling device: 18-gauge coaxial core needle biopsy system
Number of cores submitted: 4 cores, ranging from 1.0 to 1.5 cm in length

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Specimen A: Received in formalin, labeled "left upper lobe biopsy," are four cylindrical tan-white to tan-pink cores of tissue measuring in aggregate approximately 5.8 cm in combined length and ranging from 0.1 to 0.15 cm in diameter. The cores are firm and slightly pale relative to normal pulmonary parenchyma, with no grossly identified hemorrhage or necrosis. All tissue is entirely submitted in four cassettes (A1 through A4), each containing one core embedded in parallel.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
The sections show cores of lung parenchyma extensively replaced and infiltrated by a malignant epithelial neoplasm. The tumor displays a predominantly acinar growth pattern, characterized by well-formed irregular glandular structures and tubules lined by atypical cuboidal to columnar epithelial cells with moderate to abundant cytoplasm. Approximately 60% of the sampled tumor demonstrates this acinar architecture. The remaining 40% of the tumor exhibits a solid growth pattern composed of sheets of neoplastic cells with large, vesicular nuclei, prominent nucleoli, and frequent mitotic figures (up to 5 per 10 high-power fields), without identifiable mucin production by mucicarmine staining.

The neoplastic cells demonstrate marked nuclear pleomorphism with irregular nuclear contours, coarse chromatin, and conspicuous eosinophilic nucleoli. Cytoplasm is eosinophilic to amphophilic and focally vacuolated. Nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio is increased. Tumor cells show focal lepidic spread along alveolar walls at the peripheral edges of the cores, though lepidic growth is not the predominant pattern.

Desmoplastic stromal reaction is prominent, with dense fibrotic stroma surrounding the neoplastic glands. Focal areas of necrosis are present within the solid component. Perineural invasion is identified in one core. Lymphovascular invasion is focally present and suspicious in two cores. No sarcomatoid or pleomorphic differentiation is identified.

The background lung parenchyma away from the tumor demonstrates anthracotic pigment deposition, mild emphysematous changes, and respiratory bronchiolitis consistent with smoking history.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSIS:

A. LEFT UPPER LOBE LUNG MASS, CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:
   INVASIVE ADENOCARCINOMA, ACINAR PREDOMINANT (approximately 60%) WITH SOLID COMPONENT (approximately 40%)
   - GRADE: POORLY DIFFERENTIATED (GRADE 3) per IASLC/WHO grading criteria (solid predominant component >20%)
   - See immunohistochemistry and ancillary studies below
   - SPECIMEN ADEQUACY: Adequate for ancillary molecular profiling

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on Specimen A):

The following immunohistochemical stains were performed on representative sections and interpreted in conjunction with appropriate positive and negative controls:

  TTF-1 (thyroid transcription factor-1, clone SP141):     POSITIVE (strong, diffuse nuclear staining, >90% of tumor cells)
  Napsin-A (clone TMU-Ad02):                               POSITIVE (moderate to strong cytoplasmic granular staining, ~75% of tumor cells)
  CK7 (clone OV-TL 12/30):                                POSITIVE (strong, diffuse cytoplasmic staining)
  CK20 (clone Ks20.8):                                    NEGATIVE
  p40 (clone BC28):                                       NEGATIVE
  p63 (clone 4A4):                                        NEGATIVE
  Synaptophysin:                                           NEGATIVE
  Chromogranin A:                                          NEGATIVE

INTERPRETATION: The immunophenotype (TTF-1+, Napsin-A+, CK7+, CK20-, p40-, p63-) is consistent with primary lung adenocarcinoma. Squamous cell carcinoma and neuroendocrine differentiation are excluded by negative p40, p63, synaptophysin, and chromogranin A.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES:

1. PD-L1 IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (22C3 pharmDx, Dako):
   Tumor Proportion Score (TPS): 15%
   Interpretation: LOW POSITIVE (TPS 1-49%)
   Combined Positive Score (CPS): 18
   Note: Staining performed on representative sections with adequate tumor cellularity (>100 viable tumor cells evaluated).

2. ALK IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (D5F3 CDx, Ventana):
   Result: NEGATIVE (no cytoplasmic granular staining in tumor cells)
   Interpretation: ALK rearrangement not detected by IHC

3. ROS1 IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (D4D6, Cell Signaling Technology):
   Result: NEGATIVE (no cytoplasmic staining in tumor cells)
   Note: FISH confirmation for ROS1 rearrangement recommended per institutional protocol; see below.

4. ROS1 FISH (Vysis LSI ROS1 Dual Color Break-Apart Rearrangement Probe):
   Result: NEGATIVE -- No ROS1 gene rearrangement detected. Normal hybridization signal pattern observed (2 fusion signals) in >95% of tumor cells evaluated (minimum 50 cells counted).

5. EGFR MUTATION ANALYSIS (Cobas EGFR Mutation Test v2, PCR-based):
   Result: EGFR EXON 21 POINT MUTATION DETECTED -- L858R (c.2573T>G)
   Sensitizing mutation: YES
   T790M (exon 20): NOT DETECTED
   Exon 19 deletion: NOT DETECTED
   Exon 18 mutations (G719X): NOT DETECTED
   Exon 20 insertions: NOT DETECTED
   Note: Result based on extracted DNA from formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded core biopsy material. Tumor content estimated at 35-40% in section used for molecular extraction.

6. COMPREHENSIVE MOLECULAR PROFILING (NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING):
   Status: ORDERED -- PENDING
   Platform: [Institutional NGS panel / Foundation Medicine FoundationOne CDx]
   Anticipated turnaround time: 10-14 business days from date of specimen receipt in the molecular diagnostics laboratory
   Note: Specimen has been forwarded to the molecular pathology laboratory. Results will be reported as an addendum to this report upon availability.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATHOLOGIC STAGING (pTNM, AJCC 8th edition -- BIOPSY ONLY, NOT RESECTION):
Note: Formal pathologic staging cannot be assigned from a biopsy specimen alone. Clinical staging by multidisciplinary assessment: cT3 N3 M0, Stage IIIB (per radiologic and clinical correlation as provided).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT:
The morphologic and immunohistochemical findings are diagnostic of invasive adenocarcinoma of the lung, acinar predominant pattern with a significant solid component. The solid component (40%) confers poorly differentiated (Grade 3) status per current IASLC/WHO classification. The immunophenotype strongly supports a primary pulmonary origin; metastatic adenocarcinoma from extrapulmonary sites (breast, gastrointestinal, renal) is effectively excluded by the TTF-1 and Napsin-A positivity in this clinical context.

A sensitizing EGFR exon 21 L858R point mutation has been confirmed by PCR-based testing on this specimen, a finding of direct therapeutic relevance. PD-L1 TPS of 15% falls in the low positive category. ALK and ROS1 rearrangements are not identified by IHC and FISH respectively. Comprehensive NGS is pending and a supplemental report will be issued.

Specimen adequacy is confirmed as satisfactory for molecular profiling purposes based on tumor cellularity and nucleic acid yield from extracted material.

Correlation with clinical, radiologic, and other laboratory findings is recommended.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SIGNED BY:
[Attending Pathologist, MD, Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine]
Electronic signature applied

REVIEWED BY:
[Senior Resident / Fellow, Surgical Pathology]

** ADDENDUM PENDING: Comprehensive NGS panel results **
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
END OF REPORT
